prerequisites.note: The Platform Admin Portal frontend does not exist yet — it is planned for Epic E13b. This test plan is a specification for E2E tests once the Platform Admin Portal is built. E3b stories are all backend infrastructure (Platform API server, Tenant Management API, AI Gateway package, Platform Client SDK, Plan & Billing Management). Tests assume a running Platform API at :3001, a Vite dev server for the Platform Admin Portal at :5174, a seeded Platform database with test platform users, and optionally Redis at :6379.

story_coverage.E3b.1: Journeys j01-j07 cover platform admin login with MFA, health monitoring, platform user management, and authorization. AC #1 (separate server) is infrastructure, tested indirectly. AC #2 (login+MFA) tested directly via j01-j02. AC #3 (MFA mandatory) tested via j03. AC #4 (health) tested via j04. AC #5 (internal service token) is backend-only, no frontend test. AC #6 (audit) tested via j28.
story_coverage.E3b.2: Journeys j08-j16 cover tenant CRUD, lifecycle state transitions, module overrides, and feature flags. AC #1-#10 tested directly through tenant management UI flows.
story_coverage.E3b.3: No direct frontend test coverage — AI Gateway is a backend package (packages/ai-gateway) with no admin UI. AI quota check/record endpoints are internal service-to-service. AI usage dashboard is tested via j25 (quota viewing). Provider adapters, fallback chains, circuit breaker are backend-only.
story_coverage.E3b.4: No direct frontend test coverage — Platform Client SDK is a backend package (packages/platform-client). Webhook listener (POST /webhooks/platform) is backend-only. Cache invalidation is tested indirectly via tenant lifecycle and plan assignment journeys.
story_coverage.E3b.5: Journeys j17-j27 cover plan CRUD, plan assignment, billing status, billing enforcement, and AI quota management. AC #1-#6 tested directly through plan and billing management UI flows.

platform_admin.description: Primary platform admin with MFA enabled — has full access to all platform admin operations

platform_viewer.description: Read-only platform viewer — can view tenants, plans, billing but cannot create/modify

platform_admin_no_mfa.description: Platform admin without MFA enabled — login should be blocked per BR-PLT-018

seeded_data.platform_audit_log_records: Created by seed script (admin user creation, plan creation, tenant creation)

journeys.name: Platform Admin Login with MFA
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN user with MFA enabled can login with email, password, and TOTP code. A platform JWT is issued and the user is redirected to the Platform Admin dashboard.

steps.expected_result: Platform Admin Portal login page loads
steps.visual_check: Login page with PLATFORM ADMIN branding, dark sidebar theme, email and password fields, and a Sign In button. Distinct from the ERP login page.

steps.expected_result: MFA verification step appears — TOTP code input field displayed
steps.visual_check: MFA verification screen with a 6-digit TOTP code input field and a Verify button. Message indicates 'Enter the code from your authenticator app'.

steps.expected_result: Login succeeds, redirected to Platform Admin dashboard
steps.visual_check: Platform Admin dashboard loaded with dark sidebar. Navigation shows: Tenants, Plans, Users, Monitoring sections. User name 'admin@nexa-platform.local' displayed in top bar with PLATFORM_ADMIN role badge.

journeys.name: Platform Admin Login with Wrong MFA Code
journeys.description: Verify that an incorrect TOTP code is rejected with a 401 error and the user cannot proceed.

steps.visual_check: Error message displayed: 'Invalid MFA code' or 'Authentication failed'. User remains on MFA verification screen. Dashboard is NOT accessible.

journeys.name: PLATFORM_ADMIN Without MFA is Blocked
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN account without MFA enabled is blocked from logging in per BR-PLT-018 (mandatory MFA for PLATFORM_ADMIN).

steps.visual_check: Error message displayed: 'MFA required for PLATFORM_ADMIN accounts. Please set up multi-factor authentication.' Login form remains visible. Dashboard is NOT accessible.

journeys.name: View Platform Health Status
journeys.description: Verify that the health monitoring page shows Platform API status including database connectivity, Redis availability, and uptime.

steps.visual_check: Health monitoring dashboard showing: Overall status (ok/degraded), Platform API version, Uptime counter, Database connectivity indicator (connected/disconnected with latency in ms), Redis availability indicator (connected/disconnected with latency in ms). Green indicators for healthy services.

steps.expected_result: Platform health status shows 'ok' when all services are healthy
Database shows 'connected: true' with latency value in milliseconds

journeys.name: List Platform Admin Users
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN can view the list of platform admin accounts.

steps.visual_check: Platform Users list showing admin accounts. Table columns: Email, Display Name, Role (PLATFORM_ADMIN/PLATFORM_VIEWER), MFA Status (enabled/disabled), Active status. At least 3 seeded users visible.

journeys.name: Create a New Platform Admin User
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN can create a new platform admin account with email, display name, role, and password.

steps.visual_check: Success toast: 'Platform user created successfully'. Users list now shows the new user 'newadmin@nexa-platform.local' with role PLATFORM_VIEWER. PlatformAuditLog entry created (verifiable via audit log page).

journeys.name: PLATFORM_VIEWER Cannot Create Users
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_VIEWER cannot create, update, or manage platform users — receives 403 Forbidden.

steps.expected_result: Users page loads but Create button is hidden or disabled
steps.visual_check: Users list visible (PLATFORM_VIEWER can view). Create User button is either hidden or disabled. No edit/update actions available on user rows.

journeys.name: Create a New Tenant
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN can create a new tenant with code, display name, plan assignment, and database configuration. Tenant is created in PROVISIONING status.

steps.visual_check: Tenants list page with filter controls (status, plan, search) and data table. Columns: Code, Display Name, Status, Plan, Region, Last Activity, Active Modules. Create Tenant button visible for PLATFORM_ADMIN.

Create tenant form with fields: Code (slug), Display Name, Legal Name, Plan (dropdown with Core/Pro/Enterprise), Region, Database Host, Database Name. Code field shows format hint (alphanumeric + hyphens, 3-50 chars).

Success toast: 'Tenant created successfully'. Redirected to tenant detail page showing: Code='test-acme-corp', Status='PROVISIONING' (with amber badge), Plan='Professional Plan', Region='uk-south'. Activate button visible to transition to ACTIVE.

journeys.name: List and Filter Tenants
journeys.description: Verify that the tenant list supports filtering by status, plan, and search term, with pagination.

steps.expected_result: Tenants list loads with all tenants
Table refreshes showing only ACTIVE tenants
steps.visual_check: Tenant list shows only tenants with ACTIVE status badge. Pagination meta reflects filtered count. All visible rows have green 'ACTIVE' status badge.

steps.expected_result: Table refreshes showing tenants matching 'acme' search within ACTIVE status
steps.visual_check: Filtered list shows only ACTIVE tenants with 'acme' in code, display name, or legal name. Pagination reflects combined filter count.

journeys.name: View Tenant Detail with Tabs
journeys.description: Verify that the tenant detail page shows comprehensive information across multiple tabs: overview, modules, users, AI usage, billing, and audit.

steps.expected_result: Tenant detail page loads for dev-tenant
steps.visual_check: Tenant detail page with overview tab active. Shows: Code='dev-tenant', Display Name='Development Tenant', Status='ACTIVE' (green badge), Plan='Professional Plan', Region='uk-south', Last Activity timestamp. Tab navigation: Overview, Modules, Users, AI Usage, Billing, Audit.
Modules tab showing a list of plan-enabled modules with toggle switches for module overrides. Enabled modules from plan listed. Override controls available for PLATFORM_ADMIN.

steps.expected_result: Billing tab shows billing status and enforcement
steps.visual_check: Billing tab showing: Billing Status (CURRENT/GRACE/OVERDUE/BLOCKED), Enforcement Action (NONE/WARNING/READ_ONLY/SUSPENDED), Stripe Customer ID, Subscription Status, Current Period End, Grace Period Days, Last Payment Date, Dunning Level.

journeys.name: Activate a Provisioning Tenant
journeys.description: Verify that a PROVISIONING tenant can be activated (transitioned to ACTIVE status). Tests the PROVISIONING -> ACTIVE state transition.

steps.expected_result: Tenant detail page loads showing PROVISIONING status

steps.visual_check: Success toast: 'Tenant activated successfully'. Status badge changes from amber 'PROVISIONING' to green 'ACTIVE'. Activate button is replaced by lifecycle action buttons: Suspend, and settings update options.

journeys.name: Suspend an Active Tenant
journeys.description: Verify that an ACTIVE tenant can be suspended with a mandatory reason. Tests the ACTIVE -> SUSPENDED state transition and webhook push.

steps.expected_result: Tenant detail page loads showing ACTIVE status
Suspension dialog appears with mandatory reason field
steps.visual_check: Suspension dialog with a reason text field (mandatory), warning message about the impact of suspension (tenant users will lose access), and Confirm Suspend / Cancel buttons.

Success toast: 'Tenant suspended'. Status badge changes from green 'ACTIVE' to red 'SUSPENDED'. Available actions now show: Reactivate, Archive. Suspension reason visible in tenant detail. tenant.suspended webhook pushed to ERP.

journeys.name: Reactivate a Suspended Tenant
journeys.description: Verify that a SUSPENDED tenant can be reactivated (returned to ACTIVE status). Tests the SUSPENDED -> ACTIVE state transition.

steps.expected_result: Tenant detail page loads showing SUSPENDED status

steps.visual_check: Success toast: 'Tenant reactivated'. Status badge changes from red 'SUSPENDED' to green 'ACTIVE'. Available actions return to: Suspend. tenant.reactivated webhook pushed to ERP.

journeys.name: Archive a Suspended Tenant (Irrecoverable)
journeys.description: Verify that a SUSPENDED tenant can be archived. ARCHIVED is terminal — no further state transitions are possible (BR-PLT-003).

steps.expected_result: Tenant detail page loads showing SUSPENDED status
Destructive action confirmation dialog appears with strong warning
steps.visual_check: Archive confirmation dialog with red/destructive styling. Warning: 'This action is irreversible. The tenant will be permanently archived and cannot be reactivated.' Requires typing tenant code to confirm (e.g., 'Type test-acme-corp to confirm').

Success toast: 'Tenant archived'. Status badge shows grey 'ARCHIVED'. No lifecycle action buttons available (no reactivate, no suspend). Tenant detail is read-only. tenant.archived webhook pushed to ERP.

journeys.name: Invalid State Transition Rejected (ACTIVE -> ARCHIVED)
journeys.description: Verify that invalid state transitions are rejected with a 422 INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION error per BR-PLT-001. ACTIVE cannot transition directly to ARCHIVED.

steps.expected_result: Tenant detail page loads showing ACTIVE status
Archive button is NOT visible for ACTIVE tenant — only Suspend is available. The UI should prevent invalid transitions by only showing valid action buttons.
steps.visual_check: Tenant detail for ACTIVE tenant shows only the 'Suspend' action button. No 'Archive' or 'Reactivate' buttons are visible. This prevents the ACTIVE -> ARCHIVED invalid transition at the UI level.

journeys.name: Configure Tenant Module Overrides
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN can toggle module overrides for a tenant, which pushes tenant.modules_changed webhook to ERP.

steps.expected_result: Modules tab shows current module configuration
steps.visual_check: Module overrides panel showing plan-included modules with toggle switches. Each module row shows: Module Key (e.g., 'finance', 'sales', 'crm'), Enabled status (toggle), Override reason field.

steps.target: Toggle switch for 'manufacturing' module to enable it
steps.expected_result: Manufacturing module toggled on as override

steps.visual_check: Success toast: 'Module overrides updated'. Manufacturing module now shows as enabled with override reason. tenant.modules_changed webhook pushed to ERP for cache invalidation.

journeys.name: Create a New Subscription Plan
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN can create a new subscription plan with all required fields: code, display name, limits, AI quota, and enabled modules.

steps.visual_check: Plans list showing existing plans (Core, Professional, Enterprise). Table columns: Code, Display Name, Max Users, Max Companies, AI Token Allowance, Hard Limit, Modules, Rate Limit. Create Plan button visible.
Success toast: 'Plan created successfully'. Plans list now shows the new 'Startup Plan' with code='startup', maxUsers=5, enabledModules showing 4 modules. PlatformAuditLog entry created.

journeys.name: Duplicate Plan Code Returns 409
journeys.description: Verify that creating a plan with a duplicate code returns a 409 Conflict error.

steps.visual_check: Error toast or inline error: 'A plan with code core already exists' or '409 Conflict'. Form remains visible with the entered data. No duplicate plan created.

journeys.name: Update an Existing Plan
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN can update plan limits and modules on an existing plan.

steps.target: Edit button on the 'Startup Plan' row
steps.expected_result: Edit plan form loads with current values

steps.visual_check: Success toast: 'Plan updated'. Plans list shows the updated Startup Plan with maxUsers=10 and AI allowance=100,000. PlatformAuditLog entry created.

journeys.name: Assign a New Plan to a Tenant
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN can assign a different plan to a tenant. The plan change pushes a tenant.plan_changed webhook and the new entitlements apply immediately (BR-PLT-006).

steps.expected_result: Tenant detail page loads showing current plan (Professional)

steps.visual_check: Plan assignment dialog showing current plan (Professional) and a dropdown/selector with available plans. Shows comparison of old vs new limits (maxUsers, maxCompanies, AI allowance, enabled modules).

Success toast: 'Plan changed to Enterprise'. Tenant detail now shows Plan='Enterprise Plan'. Limits updated: maxUsers=500, maxCompanies=50, AI allowance=5,000,000. tenant.plan_changed webhook pushed. PlatformAuditLog entry shows old plan 'pro' -> new plan 'enterprise'.

journeys.name: View Tenant Billing Status
journeys.description: Verify that the billing status for a tenant is displayed with all relevant fields: Stripe info, subscription status, enforcement action, and dunning level.

steps.visual_check: Billing tab showing all fields: Billing Status='CURRENT' (green), Enforcement Action='NONE', Stripe Customer ID, Subscription Status, Current Period End date, Grace Period Days (14), Last Payment Date, Dunning Level (0). Enforcement controls section with 'Change Enforcement' button for PLATFORM_ADMIN.

journeys.name: Escalate Billing Enforcement (NONE -> WARNING -> READ_ONLY)
journeys.description: Verify the billing enforcement state machine: escalate from NONE to WARNING, then WARNING to READ_ONLY. Each transition pushes a billing.enforcement_changed webhook and creates audit log entries.

steps.expected_result: Billing tab loads showing Enforcement Action = NONE

steps.visual_check: Success toast: 'Enforcement changed to WARNING'. Billing tab now shows: Enforcement Action='WARNING' (amber badge), Billing Status='GRACE'. billing.enforcement_changed webhook pushed.

input_data.reason: Invoice overdue by 21 days, grace period expired

steps.visual_check: Success toast: 'Enforcement changed to READ_ONLY'. Billing tab shows: Enforcement Action='READ_ONLY' (red badge), Billing Status='OVERDUE'. ERP write operations will be blocked for this tenant (BR-PLT-005).

journeys.name: Billing Enforcement Escalates to SUSPENDED
journeys.description: Verify that escalating enforcement from READ_ONLY to SUSPENDED also transitions the tenant status to SUSPENDED and pushes both billing.enforcement_changed and tenant.suspended webhooks.

steps.expected_result: Tenant detail loads showing READ_ONLY enforcement
Billing tab shows Enforcement Action = READ_ONLY

input_data.reason: Invoice overdue by 60 days, no response to dunning notices

steps.expected_result: Enforcement changed to SUSPENDED, tenant status also changes
steps.visual_check: Success toast: 'Enforcement changed to SUSPENDED'. Billing tab shows: Enforcement Action='SUSPENDED' (dark red badge), Billing Status='BLOCKED'. Tenant overview tab shows Status changed to 'SUSPENDED'. Both billing.enforcement_changed and tenant.suspended webhooks pushed.

journeys.name: Invalid Billing Enforcement Transition Rejected
journeys.description: Verify that skipping enforcement levels (NONE -> READ_ONLY directly) is rejected with a 422 INVALID_ENFORCEMENT_TRANSITION error.

steps.expected_result: Tenant detail loads showing NONE enforcement
Only valid transitions are available. From NONE, only WARNING should be selectable. READ_ONLY and SUSPENDED should be disabled or hidden.
steps.visual_check: Enforcement change dialog shows only 'WARNING' as an available option from current NONE state. READ_ONLY and SUSPENDED are greyed out/disabled. This prevents invalid enforcement transitions at the UI level.

journeys.name: View Tenant AI Quota
journeys.description: Verify that the AI quota for a tenant is displayed with usage percentage, allowance, soft/hard limits, and period dates.

steps.expected_result: AI Usage tab loads with quota information
steps.visual_check: AI Usage tab showing: Token Allowance (from plan), Tokens Used, Usage Percentage with progress bar, Soft Limit % threshold, Hard Limit % threshold, Burst Allowance, Period Start/End dates, Plan code. Progress bar colour changes based on usage (green < soft, amber between soft-hard, red > hard).

journeys.name: Update Tenant AI Quota Limits
journeys.description: Verify that a PLATFORM_ADMIN can update AI quota parameters (token allowance, soft/hard limit percentages) for a tenant.

input_data.reason: Temporary quota increase for Q1 reporting period

steps.visual_check: Success toast: 'AI quota updated'. AI Usage tab now shows: Token Allowance=1,000,000, Soft Limit=70%, Hard Limit=95%. Usage percentage recalculated based on new allowance. PlatformAuditLog entry created with change details.

journeys.name: PLATFORM_VIEWER Cannot Modify Plans, Tenants, or Billing
journeys.description: Verify that PLATFORM_VIEWER role can view tenants, plans, and billing but cannot create, update, or perform lifecycle operations. All mutation buttons are hidden or disabled.

steps.visual_check: Tenants list visible. Create Tenant button is hidden/disabled. No edit, suspend, reactivate, or archive actions available on tenant rows.

steps.target: First tenant row to view detail
steps.expected_result: Tenant detail page loads in read-only mode
steps.visual_check: Tenant detail page shows all information (overview, modules, billing, AI usage) but NO action buttons: no Suspend, no Activate, no Change Plan, no Edit Module Overrides, no Change Enforcement. All data is read-only.
Plans list visible with all plan data. Create Plan button is hidden/disabled. No edit actions available on plan rows.

journeys.name: View Platform Audit Log
journeys.description: Verify that all state-changing admin actions (login, tenant create, plan create, enforcement change) are recorded in the PlatformAuditLog and viewable through the platform audit log screen.

steps.target: Audit Log link in sidebar (under Monitoring section)
steps.visual_check: Platform Audit Log page with data table. Columns: Timestamp, Action, Target Type, Target ID, Actor (Platform User), IP Address. Records sorted by timestamp descending. Filter controls for action type, target type, date range, and actor.

steps.expected_result: At least one audit record with action='auth.login' visible (from the login that just occurred)
Multiple audit records visible from previous journeys: tenant.create, plan.created, tenant.suspend, billing.enforcement_changed, etc. Each record shows actor, action, target, and timestamp.
steps.visual_check: Audit log showing a rich history of platform admin actions. Records include: auth.login, tenant.create, tenant.activate, tenant.suspend, tenant.reactivate, tenant.archive, plan.created, plan.updated, billing.enforcement_changed, platform_user.create. All entries have complete actor, IP, and timestamp data.

Detail view showing: Action (e.g., 'tenant.suspend'), Target Type ('tenant'), Target ID (UUID), Actor (platform user email), Details (JSON showing reason, status change), IP Address, User Agent, Timestamp (ISO format).

journeys.name: Unauthenticated Access Redirects to Login
journeys.description: Verify that accessing any platform admin page without authentication redirects to the login page.

steps.visual_check: Login page displayed. No tenant data visible. URL may show /login or /login?redirect=/admin/tenants.

journeys.name: Full Tenant Lifecycle: Create -> Activate -> Assign Plan -> Enforce Billing -> Suspend -> Reactivate
journeys.description: End-to-end journey testing the complete tenant lifecycle: create tenant, activate, assign a plan, escalate billing enforcement through WARNING -> READ_ONLY, then recover via payment (NONE). Verifies state machine transitions, webhook pushes, and audit trail at each step.

steps.visual_check: Tenant detail showing Status='PROVISIONING' (amber), Plan='Core Plan'. Activate button visible.
Status changed to 'ACTIVE' (green). Suspend button now visible.

input_data.reason: Upgrade to Pro for E2E test

steps.visual_check: Plan='Professional Plan'. Limits updated. tenant.plan_changed webhook pushed.
Enforcement Action='WARNING' (amber). Billing Status='GRACE'. billing.enforcement_changed webhook pushed.
Enforcement Action='READ_ONLY' (red). Billing Status='OVERDUE'. ERP writes blocked for this tenant.

input_data.reason: E2E test: payment received, account restored

steps.visual_check: Enforcement Action='NONE' (green). Billing Status='CURRENT'. ERP writes re-enabled. billing.enforcement_changed webhook pushed.

steps.expected_result: Multiple audit records visible for the lifecycle test tenant
steps.visual_check: Audit log showing chronological entries for the E2E lifecycle test: tenant.create, tenant.activate, tenant.plan_changed (core -> pro), billing.enforcement_changed (NONE->WARNING, WARNING->READ_ONLY, READ_ONLY->NONE). Each entry has complete actor, target, and timestamp data.
